ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #5, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Apr-29-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter was heard with case number PCN-01-281337 Conservatorship of Mary Cusack.

Appearances: Ernest Der Court appointed Counsel for Mary Cusack. Janet Boessenecker for and with Linda Harrington (Petitioner).

Ms. Boessenecker and Ms. Harrington explained that the caregivers were being paid by bill pay (automatic payment system) on a regular basis, that bills were presented to Ms. Harrington in addition to the automatic payments during the time she was dealing with medical issues and that they received a check from Health Professionals for the exact amount of the overpayment. Mr. Der stated that he was satisfied as long as the overpayment was credited back. Both accountings were taken under submission.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #5, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Apr-18-2011 Continued To Apr-25-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: This matter was heard with case number PCN-01-281337 Conservatorhsip of Mary K. Cusack.

Appearances: Janet Boessenecker for Linda Harrington.

Ms. Boessenecker explained why Ms. Harrington was not present and stated that she was not able to contact her. The Court stated that it wants Ms. Harrington to be present and continued both accounting petitions to April 25, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If Ms. Boesenecker's Client is not available, she is to advise the Probate Examiner as soon as possible.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy W. Yee.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #5, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Mar-07-2011 Continued To Apr-18-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number PCN-01-281337 (Conservatorship of Mary K. Cusack).

Appearances: Janet Boessenecker for and with Linda Harrington.

The Court stated that it wanted to focus on the the conservatorship and payments to the health professionals, that the payments to the health care professionals seem to change, and that the bottom line is that the Court needs the ledgers from the health professionals. Ms. Boessenecker addressed the fluctuation regarding the payments. The Court stated that it wants to obtain the right amount at the beginning of this account period which is from 7-1-08 through 6-30-10. The Court requested the ledger beginning 7-1-08, stated that the current statement shows that there is a credit, that it wants to know where the credit came from, and that it doesn't want to focus on anything after this account period which will be addressed next time. The Court further stated that the ledger should have three columns listing what was billed, what was paid, and then the credit/balance. The Court continued both matters to April 18, 2011 for receipt of the additional information regarding the medical payments. The additional information is to be on file by the close of business on April 12, 2011.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #5, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Feb-28-2011 Continued To Mar-07-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Accept Resignation Of Trustee And Appoint Successor Trustee

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #5, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jan-10-2011 Continued To Feb-28-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: This matter was heard with case number PCN-01-281337 Conservatorship of Mary K. Cusack.

Appearances: Janet Boessenecker for and with Linda Harrington.

Ms. Boessenecker stated that the Petitioner filed a declaration this morning on the Trust matter. The Court directed Ms. Harrington to revise the portion of schedule C showing the Health Professionals' payments so that the amounts correspond to the dates. The Court stated that it was statisfied that Linda Harrington's license is an active license and reinstated Ms. Harrington's powers as Conservator and reinstated powers in the Trust matter. Ms. Boessenecker stated that Debra Dolch was in favor of taking the positions as Successor Conservator and Succssor Trustee, and that she would file such petitions. The matter was continued to February 28, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The revised schedule C is due by February 9, 2011.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #5, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Dec-13-2010 Continued To Jan-10-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: This matter was heard with case number PCN-01-281337 Conservatorship of Mary Cusack.

Appearances: Janet Boessenecker for and with Linda Harrington (Conservator/Trustee).

Ms. Boessenecker and Ms. Harrington addressed all of the issues raised in the Examiner's notes with regard to the Conservatorship and the Trust matters. The Court directed Ms. Boessenecker to file a declaration reflecting all of the responses/explanations provided in open Court with exhibits (invoices, contracts, letters, etc.) to support such explanations which is due by January 5, 2011. The Court suspended Linda Harrington's powers as of today and will appoint Counsel to represent Ms. Cusack. The Court also directed Linda Harrington to write a letter to Mary Quinn (Probate Court Director) explaining the status of her professional fiduciary license. The accounting petitions were continued to January 10, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #4, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar May-07-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Janet Boessenecker for Linda Harrington.

Ms. Boessenecker stated that the vacant unit was a small office space, that it had been damaged in a fire, that it had been cleaned up but it was not habitable and that there were no grounds to get a new tenant in until the lease expires at the end of the year. In addition, she stated that it has been vacant since 2005, that it only generated $200 a month in rent when it was rented, and that the tenant in the major space wanted to refurbish the office and rent it out. Furthermore, she addressed the contract for attorney fees stating that counsel is kept on to assist with small issues that come up with the property. The Court suggested that in the contract a cap be put on the amount of fees that can be requested. The request by the Trustee to increase the monthly fee from $500 to $750 is granted. The Court approved the accounting, (subject to review of the order).

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda/Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.
